item.title: Home Buyers Hammered as War-Fueled Bond Rout Drives Rates Higher
item.description: Najimah Roberson, a lifelong renter, spent the past two years searching around Harrisburg, Pennsylvania, for a home she could afford — getting outbid nearly 30 times along the way.

item.title: SpaceX Set to Launch Starship Rocket in Key Test of IPO Plans
item.description: SpaceX is poised to launch its gargantuan Starship rocket on its twelfth major test flight on Thursday, part of a high-stakes mission as the Elon Musk-led company preps for the largest initial public offering in history.

item.title: IMF Cuts France Growth Forecast and Warns of High Uncertainty
item.description: The International Monetary Fund lowered its growth forecast for France’s economy this year as the shock of the Iran war hits activity and uncertainty builds ahead of next year’s presidential elections.

item.title: Stock Futures Fall as Nvidia Fails to Spark Lift; Walmart Slides
item.description: US equity futures retreated while oil and Treasury yields climbed as traders awaited concrete signals on progress toward a Middle East peace deal, with Nvidia Corp.’s earnings doing little to reignite momentum in the artificial-intelligence trade.

item.title: Turkey Liquidated Almost All Its US Treasury Holdings in March
item.description: Turkey offloaded almost all of its US Treasuries in March as it stepped up efforts to support its currency during the first month of the Iran war, according to Bloomberg calculations based on US Treasury data.

item.title: US Jobless Claims Were Little Changed at 209,000 Last Week
item.description: Applications for US unemployment benefits were little changed last week, signaling layoffs remain muted despite recent job-cut announcements.

item.title: US Housing Starts Decline on Drop in Single-Family Homes
item.description: US housing starts declined in April as construction of single-family homes dropped by the most in nearly a year, suggesting builders are growing cautious amid higher mortgage rates.

item.title: Blockchain.com Files Confidentially for Initial Public Offering
item.description: Blockchain.com Group Holdings Inc., one of the oldest crypto exchanges, has filed confidentially with the US Securities and Exchange Commission for an initial public offering, joining the spate of digital asset companies seeking to go public amid one of the most turbulent periods for the sector.

item.title: Fertilizer Crunch in Brazil Raises Risks for Farm Economy
item.description: A spike in fertilizer costs driven by the Iran war is hitting farmers in agriculture powerhouse Brazil at the worst possible time, underscoring how the Middle East conflict is threatening global food supplies.

item.title: Anthropic’s New Consulting Venture Makes Its First Acquisition
item.description: The new artificial intelligence enterprise services firm backed by Blackstone Inc., Anthropic PBC and Hellman & Friedman has picked a San Francisco firm called Fractional AI as the operational centerpiece of the as-yet unnamed venture.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for May 21, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures fall 0.3% as of 7:46 a.m. in New York as traders continue to follow the situation in the Middle East.

item.title: Reeves Cuts VAT on Summer Attractions to Ease Cost of Living
item.description: Chancellor of the Exchequer Rachel Reeves will cut value-added tax on British summer attractions, part of a package of measures aimed at reducing the cost of living for voters amid the economic fallout of war in the Middle East.

item.title: Dangote’s Refinery Private Placement Draws $2 Billion of Demand
item.description: Aliko Dangote said a private placement of shares in his oil refinery in Nigeria attracted as much as $2 billion of investor demand before a proposed initial public offering.

item.title: French Resilience to War Fallout Wilts in Blow to Budget Efforts
item.description: France’s economic sturdiness in the face of the Iran war shock is crumbling, jeopardizing plans to rein in the budget deficit as the country gears up for a pivotal election.

item.title: Indonesia’s Audacious Commodities Shakeup Puts Traders On Edge
item.description: The palm oil, coal and nickel industries are trying to understand what the president’s overhaul will entail.

item.title: The Microeconomic Hit From ICE Raids: Bellwether Firms Get Double Whammy
item.description: The immigration crackdown is causing a major drag on some micro economies in America.

item.title: Nvidia’s Huang Tries to Get Investors to See the Future of AI
item.description: The chipmaker’s CEO argues another big wave of AI demand is coming

item.title: ICE Raids Did Lasting Damage to American Businesses
item.description: In one corner of Charlotte, foot traffic and sales remain depressed six months after deportation raids.

item.title: The AI Boom Is Helping Asia Exporters Weather the Energy Shock
item.description: Chip exports from South Korea and Japan are surging, helping offset headwinds from high fuel costs

item.title: Microsoft and EY to Spend $1 Billion to Help Clients Adopt AI
item.description: Technology giant Microsoft Corp. and consulting firm EY are partnering to spend more than $1 billion to encourage their clients to launch major artificial intelligence projects.

item.title: EU to Seek Carve-Out for Banned China Chips to Shield Auto Firms
item.description: The European Union will propose temporarily lifting sanctions on a Chinese semiconductor supplier after automakers warned of impending supply chain chaos if the ban isn’t removed.

item.title: Hungarian Budget Data Was ‘Falsified,’ Premier Magyar Warns
item.description: Hungary will probably have a “very high” budget shortfall this year, Prime Minister Peter Magyar said, adding that Viktor Orban’s administration had likely “falsified” data.

item.title: Repeat After Me, Stocks Are Not an Effective Inflation Hedge
item.description: Surveys indicate that investors are piling into stocks even as they see rising inflation. That’s likely to backfire as equities aren’t the hedge against price growth they are often taken to be.

item.title: Vietnam’s Convicted Mogul Repays $455 Million to Bondholders
item.description: Vietnam real estate tycoon Truong My Lan, convicted in the nation’s largest fraud case, has repaid a total of more than 12 trillion dong ($455 million) to about 42,000 bond holders as the government works to clawback billions of ill-gotten gains.

item.title: SpaceX IPO Could Be Just the Start of the Mega Listings
item.description: OpenAI, Anthropic are gearing up for listings later this year.

item.title: White House Backs IBM’s $2 Billion Quantum Computing Foundry
item.description: The Trump administration has agreed to award a $1 billion grant to International Business Machines Corp. to build a foundry for producing quantum computing chips, part of a broad strategy to build up the emerging industry and expand the country’s technology leadership.

item.title: Stockholm Index Briefly Halted as New Glitch Hits Nasdaq Nordics
item.description: Stockholm’s blue-chip share index was disrupted by a technical glitch on Thursday, the second time in less than a month that Nasdaq Inc. has suffered problems with its Nordic benchmarks.

item.title: Ford Is the Latest Old Economy Company Caught Up in the AI Boom
item.description: AI is giving some old-school manufacturers a profit boost.

item.title: Deere Gets Lift From Construction as Farmers Still Struggle
item.description: Deere & Co. is getting a boost from its construction and forestry segments, helping to offset volatile agriculture markets that continue to crimp farmer spending.

item.title: UK Must Harness Flexible Grids Before Upgrades to Curb Bills
item.description: The UK’s energy regulator said distribution grids must use flexibility tools such as smart technology to better control demand before being allowed to make network investments that add to customers’ bills.
